address: Tudor House, Hampton Court Road, East Molesey, Surrey KT8
description: Characterful Grade II listed home backing onto Bushy Park. Description Set behind a gated wall, Tudor House is a delightful period home dating back to the late 1600s and reputedly designed by an assistant to famous architect Sir Christopher Wren and built for horticulturalist Abraham Fish who was the gardener in chief for joint monarchs William and Mary. The property which has been recently renovated by the owners, enjoys a picturesque courtyard garden to the front with an array of plants and shrubbery; the perfect place for al fresco dining. Welcoming you into this delightful home is the dining area featuring a beautiful stone floor, feature fireplace and bay window. Adjacent is the kitchen which offers a good range of dark blue wall and base mounted cabinetry, granite worktops and integrated appliances. From the dining area, a wooden staircase leads up to the first floor from where stunning views of Hampton Court Palace and the Royal gardens can be enjoyed. This floor comprises a cosy living room as well as bedroom two which offers flexibility for use as a study featuring wood panelled walls and a feature fireplace. The stairs continue to the second floor which similarly enjoys the views over the Palace and grounds. There are two bedrooms on this floor and a well-appointed bathroom with bath and separate shower. Location Tudor House is situated in a fantastic position backing Bushy Park and enjoying magnificent views of the Royal gardens of Hampton Court Palace opposite. The River Thames is also close by providing scenic towpath walks. Hampton Court Station is 0.6 miles away and provides regular and direct trains into London Waterloo from 33 minutes. Bridge Road, located in popular 'Hampton Court Village', is 0.8 miles away and offers an array of restaurants, bars, boutiques and shops. More extensive shopping can be found in nearby Kingston upon Thames (1.4 miles). The road links are excellent with the M3 being just 4 miles away which links with the M25 and M4 and London Heathrow airport is located about 10 miles away. Square Footage: 1,217 sq f

description: Ruth Conlon of Keller Williams is delighted to present this beautiful home in an award-winning development, which won the What House Gold Award for Best House 2016. The project ('The Longfords') from Q Developments was created in partnership with 5Plus Architects and was designed to create high-quality, sustainable homes that blend harmoniously with the surrounding neighbourhood. The judges described the homes as 'tardis-like', and praised the well-planned, light-filled living spaces and the seamless flow between them. They highlighted the clever use of timber screens on the front elevations, the striking roof light above the stairs, and the thoughtful colour palette throughout. Nestled within a small, gated mews of three homes, this stunning end-of-terrace contemporary four-bedroom house offers a tranquil retreat with a garden, terraces, off-street parking, and secure entry behind a gatehouse. The gated development, with CCTV, provides peace of mind, making it ideal for frequent travellers or those seeking a secure environment. Entering the home, the light-filled hallway feels spacious and includes a large cloakroom. It leads into an open-plan kitchen/dining/reception room that flows effortlessly into the garden and terrace. The kitchen, designed to be the heart of the home, boasts modern fittings, including two large fridge freezers, two ovens, and a multi-functional tap with instant hot and filtered water. Whether youâre enjoying the breakfast bar or relaxing by the bio-ethanol fireplace, this space is perfect for everyday living. A front section of the room could serve as a cosy reading corner or even accommodate a dining table or piano, as it once did for the current owner. Downstairs, the oak staircase leads to a versatile family room or guest suite with fitted wardrobes, opening onto a second terrace connected to the main garden. This space offers endless possibilities-ideal as an office, playroom, games room, or hobby room. The lower level also features a full-sized bathroom, utility room, and generous pantry or storage room. It could even function as independent living quarters, with its own separate side access for added convenience.The space is constructed with waterproof caltite reinforced concrete and includes a secondary cavity drainage system for added durability. On the top floor, the master suite offers a peaceful retreat with a view of the garden, with a small terrace, fitted wardrobes, and an en-suite shower room. A family bathroom, second bedroom, and third single bedroom, currently used as an office, complete this level. The home is equipped with modern technology, including recessed downlights with dimmer switches, sky+ cabling, TV and radio antennae, underfloor heating, and a ventilating heat recovery system. The development also features eco-friendly materials, with slate roofs, green roofs, and masonry elevations incorporating wet-cast stone assemblies. The annual service charge for the mews is approximately Â£850. EPC rating C. The location is perfect for those seeking access to both central London and the airport, with Teddington and Hampton Wick stations nearby. Whether commuting to Heathrow, Waterloo, or King's Cross, you can be there within an hour and now within the 'super-loop express' Heathrow and parts of London are more accessible than they have ever been, via Teddington. For those who enjoy nature, the historical Hampton Court and Bushy Park are just moments away, while Kingston offers excellent shopping options, including the Fenwick Centre. The exclusive Lensbury Club is also nearby, and the area is served by top-rated schools, including Collis, Hampton Wick & St John The Baptist, and secondary schools like Teddington, Grey Court, and Turing House. Teddington High Street is the heart of this charming, riverside town, offering a perfect blend of traditional village appeal and modern convenience. Lined with a variety of independent boutiques, trendy cafes, and high-end restaurants, itâs the perfect place to spend a leisurely afternoon or enjoy a night out. Whether youâre looking for unique homeware, artisanal food, or a refreshing drink in one of the cozy pubs, the High Street caters to all tastes. With its picturesque surroundings, friendly atmosphere, and easy access to the River Thames, Teddington High Street is a vibrant and welcoming hub for both locals and visitors alike. Also just a short walk away is Hampton Wick. A quaint area also offering a selection of independent shops, cafes and traditional pubs, as well as excellent transport links. description: Originally built circa 1932, during 2017 the property underwent major works to include a two storey side extension, first floor extension to include the relocation of the stairs and bathroom to the first floor with the first floor sub divided to create further bedroom accommodation in accordance with the completion certificate (reference to planning consent 17/00903/fp). The garden is mainly laid to lawn with mature bordering plants. property_name: 3 bed end terrace house for sale
description: Tucked away in one of Ham's quiet little secrets - a small lane you'd never think to wander down - this end-of-terrace Victorian cottage sits within a charming enclave that feels a touch like a country village. It's the sort of hidden pocket you only discover if you go looking. In keeping with that sense of calm, the cottage presents an unusually wide, beautifully refurbished frontage. Its generous courtyard-style front garden has been redesigned as a practical, welcoming space: Smart paving bordered by wide planted beds and a couple of lovely oaks. It's the kind of front garden you'd actually use - somewhere to sit with a coffee, let children play, or simply enjoy a bit of breathing space before stepping inside. The real surprise comes once you cross the threshold. The Victorian shell opens into a vast sweep of contemporary open-plan living, created by a bold full-width extension and flooded with daylight from six large skylights. The aesthetic is warm and pared back: Solid wood floors uniting the space, clean lines, nothing fussy. The sash windows throughout the house - newly made but faithful in proportion and style - add character and anchor the design in its period origins. Despite its scale, the ground floor feels inviting and comfortable. Thoughtful zoning creates two generous living areas where reading, working or watching TV can all coexist without crowding each other. The sleek, linear kitchen blends in seamlessly, centred around an island that feels perfectly in tune with the room's proportions. At the far end, full-width floor-to-ceiling glazing frames an unbroken view of the expansive garden beyond - a view you enjoy all day, from every corner of the space. A generous terrace runs directly outside the glass doors - an easy, natural place for gatherings, summer dinners or simply sitting out among birdsong and greenery. Beyond it, the garden stretches out further than you expect, full of mature planting, seasonal interest and a sense of open sky. The birdsong is striking - a reminder that this really is a pocket of countryside just minutes from Richmond. At the end, a well-built garden room offers flexible extra space: A gym, a studio, a generous home office - take your pick. There's even a cleverly tucked-away study/play area on the ground floor, along with a smart contemporary bathroom, making the space remarkably versatile. In truth, you really won't need to go upstairs until bedtime. When you do, you'll find three comfortable double bedrooms that continue the home's gentle simplicity, each enjoying tranquil outlooks either over the leafy garden or the courtyard frontage. A modern bathroom completes the first floor. This is a home defined by comfort, clarity and space: Victorian charm on the outside, thoughtful contemporary living within. Understated, calm and quietly impressiv

Overview This mid-terraced house offers around 86 sq m of internal space arranged over two floors, with a layout that makes good use of its footprint. Two reception rooms sit on the ground floor, with three bedrooms and a bathroom above, creating a clear separation between living and sleeping space. The rooms are well balanced, with natural light coming from both front and rear aspects. Planning permission is in place for a ground floor extension and a loft conversion, providing flexibility for future changes if needed. Living space The front reception is anchored by a wide bay window that brings in consistent natural light and gives the room a sense of width. A tiled fireplace sits centrally and reads as a feature without restricting how the room can be arranged. The proportions allow for a full seating layout, with space to move comfortably through the room. Behind it, the second reception opens directly onto the garden through glazed doors. Its simple, rectangular shape makes it easy to use day to day, whether as a dining space or an additional sitting room, and the connection to the garden gives it a practical role in the overall layout. Kitchen The kitchen is set out as a galley at the rear of the house, with units and worktops running along both sides. The arrangement is efficient and easy to work in, with good natural light from the window and glazed door. Direct access to the garden adds to its everyday practicality. Bedrooms and bathroom(s) The main bedroom occupies the front of the house and benefits from the bay window, giving it the largest footprint of the three. A full wall of fitted wardrobes provides extensive built-in storage while still leaving room for additional furniture. The second bedroom is a well-sized double overlooking the garden and feels calm and private. The third bedroom is smaller but sensibly proportioned, working well as a study, childâs room, or occasional guest space. The bathroom sits off the landing and is arranged for ease of use, with a bath and overhead shower, a separate shower enclosure, WC, and basin. A window provides natural light and ventilation. Outdoor space The rear garden is accessed directly from the house and is laid mainly to lawn, with a paved area closest to the building. Its length allows it to be used in sections, with space for seating as well as planting. Street parking is available to the front on a permit basis. Location Motspur Park station is a short walk away and offers regular rail services into central London. New Malden and Raynes Park stations are also within walking distance, providing additional route options. Local shops, everyday amenities, and bus routes are close by.
